leads.description: Building Project - AAC — Scope of work consists of a residential second-floor addition constructed above an existing garage, with associated interior modifications and required structural, mechanical, electrical, and plumbing work, all per approved plans. Demolition Perform selective interior and roof demolition only within the limits shown on the approved demolition plans to allow for the proposed second-floor addition. No demolition of primary structural elements is proposed except where specifically indicated on the approved structural drawings. Structural Construct new second-floor floor framing, wall framing, and roof framing within the footprint shown on the plans. Provide all required structural connections, headers, beams, anchors, straps, and hardware as detailed on the approved structural and foundation drawings. Any required foundation or reinforcing work shall be performed only as shown on the approved plans. Architectural Construct new habitable space above the existing garage, including exterior walls, roof assembly, windows, and doors per approved architectural drawings. Interior work includes new non-load-bearing partitions, drywall, and finishes per plans. Exterior finishes shall match or be compatible with the existing residence. Roofing Roof work is limited to the addition footprint only, including new roof framing, sheathing, underlayment, and roof covering, with proper flashing and tie-in to the existing roof system as detailed on the approved plans. No reroof of the existing residence outside the addition footprint is proposed. Mechanical (HVAC) Mechanical scope consists of ductwork extensions and air distribution modifications only to serve the new addition, per the approved mechanical plans. No new mechanical units are proposed unless specifically shown on the plans. Existing equipment shall remain. Electrical Electrical work includes new branch circuits, lighting, switches, and receptacles serving the addition only, installed in accordance with the approved electrical plans and applicable codes. No service upgrade is proposed unless indicated on the plans. Plumbing Plumbing work includes installation of new plumbing fixtures and associated water supply and sanitary drainage connections serving the addition, per the approved plumbing plans. No work is proposed beyond what is shown on the plans. Code Compliance and Inspections All work shall comply with the Florida Building Code, City of Plantation amendments, and applicable regulations. Required inspections shall be scheduled and completed at all applicable stages, including structural, rough MEP, insulation, and final inspections. Use and Occupancy There is no change in use or occupancy classification. The residence shall remain a single-family dwelling. 8920 S LAKE DASHA DR
